title: Xtra Rich Russian Beef Pie
description: A rich and savory Russian dish featuring shortcrust pastry and beef, perfect for a brunch mood, made in an iron teppan and free from onions and garlic.
instructions: Preheat the iron teppan to 180 degrees Celsius.
Roll out the shortcrust pastry to a thickness of 5mm.
In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, sliced mushrooms, butter, eggs, sour cream, dijon mustard, salt, and black pepper, and mix until just combined.
Place the pastry into the iron teppan and fill with the beef mixture.
Fold the pastry over the filling to form a pie shape.
Brush the pastry with a little water and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Bake the pie for 35 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
Remove the pie from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
